Title: Population genomics demystifies the defoliation phenotype in the plant pathogen Verticillium dahliae

Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of 75 V. dahliae strains: GenBank PRJNA171348; CNSA CNP0000156
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive lipid involved in defoliation/leaf abscission and virulence-related ABA disruption
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putatively contributes to NAE biosynthesis as a NAPE-PLD homolog, but deletion did not abolish defoliation, likely due to redundancy.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted NAPE-PLD; likely functionally redundant with duplicate orthologue VEDA_04083 in Vd991, explaining lack of clear knockout phenotype.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VdDf7 encodes a predicted NAPE-PLD homolog in the G-LSR2 cluster. 2. The study links it conceptually to hydrolysis of NAPE to NAE. 3. Single-gene deletion did not abolish defoliation, so it was not identified as a decisive determinant compared with VdDf5/VdDf6.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary target for studying fungal NAE metabolism, but redundancy limits immediate application as a standalone control point.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture supernatant; host tissues after fungal secretion
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cotton infection; in vitro culture for metabolite analysis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion; infection assays; metabolite pathway inference from annotation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Degrades NAEs to maintain host NAE homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is induced in cotton by D-strain infection, consistent with host response to elevated fungal NAEs and disruption of native NAE metabolism.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significantly upregulated in cotton roots at 5 d after inoculation with D strain Vd991 versus ND strain VDG78
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study interprets FAAH upregulation as a host response, not as a direct causal fungal determinant of NAE production.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhFAAH3 transcript increased strongly in cotton challenged with defoliating V. dahliae. 2. This supports disruption of host NAE metabolism by pathogen-derived NAE 12:0. 3. Gene was monitored as a responsive host metabolic component rather than manipulated as a core determinant.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible host-side biomarker or engineering target for modulating NAE homeostasis and disease response, though causal validation is lacking in this study.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Infection by defoliating versus nondefoliating V. dahliae strains

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Degrades NAEs to maintain host NAE homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly induced by D-strain infection and by exogenous NAE 12:0, indicating host response to elevated fungal NAE 12:0.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in cotton roots at 5 d after Vd991 infection and at 48 h after NAE 12:0 feeding
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the most responsive FAAH genes to fungal NAE and culture filtrate treatment.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhFAAH6 was strongly induced by defoliating V. dahliae infection. 2. It was also induced by exogenous NAE 12:0 treatment, supporting responsiveness to this metabolite. 3. The gene supports the model that fungal NAE 12:0 perturbs host NAE metabolism.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential host target to test whether tuning FAAH activity alters susceptibility or defoliation severity.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Defoliating strain infection; exogenous NAE 12:0; culture filtrate treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: FAAH overexpression in plants is discussed as potentially disturbing hormone balance and compromising immunity.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cotton roots under pathogen or NAE treatment

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Degrades NAEs to maintain host NAE homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly induced by D-strain infection and by exogenous NAE 12:0, indicating host response to elevated fungal NAE 12:0.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in cotton roots at 5 d after Vd991 infection and at 48 h after NAE 12:0 feeding
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Responsive host FAAH family member implicated in disrupted NAE homeostasis during defoliation-inducing interaction.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhFAAH7 was significantly induced by the defoliating strain. 2. It also responded to exogenous NAE 12:0. 3. Results support the hypothesis that fungal NAE 12:0 stimulates host FAAH response and perturbs hormone/metabolite balance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential host gene for testing NAE catabolism-mediated resistance or susceptibility mechanisms.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Defoliating strain infection; exogenous NAE 12:0; culture filtrate treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: FAAH overexpression is discussed as potentially increasing ABA sensitivity and compromising immunity.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cotton roots under pathogen or NAE treatment

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Degrades NAEs to maintain host NAE homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly induced by D-strain infection and by exogenous NAE 12:0, indicating host response to elevated fungal NAE 12:0.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in cotton roots at 5 d after Vd991 infection and at 48 h after NAE 12:0 feeding
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Culture filtrate from D strain also induced expression relative to ND strain filtrate.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhFAAH8 expression increased under D-strain infection. 2. It was also induced by NAE 12:0 feeding and D-strain culture filtrate. 3. Findings support a host metabolic response to pathogen-derived NAE overload.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential biomarker or manipulation target for studying NAE homeostasis during wilt/defoliation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Defoliating strain infection; exogenous NAE 12:0; culture filtrate treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: FAAH overexpression is discussed as potentially increasing ABA sensitivity and compromising immunity.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Cotton roots under pathogen or NAE treatment

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes overproduction of NAE 12:0 and is required for the defoliation phenotype and high virulence in cotton, olive, and okra.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plant infection of cotton, olive, and okra; culture conditions for secondary metabolite production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion reduces fungal biomass and vascular discoloration, indicating reduced virulence/adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative polyketide synthase-associated step contributing to biosynthesis of lipid-derived metabolite(s) required for NAE 12:0 accumulation; exact reaction not elucidated.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal culture supernatant; transported to host cotton roots/leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Induces defoliation/leaf abscission and contributes to virulence, possibly via ABA-related disruption.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of 75 V. dahliae strains: GenBank PRJNA171348; CNSA CNP0000156
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for biosynthesis of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly upregulated during cotton infection, especially around 2 d post-inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Single-gene deletion of VdDf5 abolished the defoliation phenotype in cotton. 2. Double deletion with VdDf6 eliminated defoliation in cotton, olive, and okra, while complementation restored it. 3. Loss of VdDf5 markedly reduced NAE 12:0 accumulation, linking the gene to biosynthesis of the defoliation-inducing metabolite.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pathogen target for disease control by disabling fungal NAE 12:0 production or monitoring D pathotype virulence determinants.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: NAE 12:0-producing branch of the G-LSR2 secondary metabolite cluster
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homolog originated from horizontal transfer donor Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. vasinfectum, but donor homolog did not confer defoliation in V. dahliae background.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout; double knockout; complementation; ectopic expression; UHPLC-MS/MS metabolite quantification; pathogenicity assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native locus in complementation; TrpC constitutive promoter used for other ectopic expression experiments, not specifically for VdDf5
